title: Constrained LLM Generation (GGUF)
cap_description: Generate text with LLGuidance constraints (JSON schema, regex, grammar). Output is NDJSON streaming.
documentation:
## Purpose

Runs autoregressive text generation with hard structural constraints using LLGuidance. The model is guaranteed to produce output that matches the supplied JSON schema, regular expression, or grammar — making this the right cap when downstream code must parse the LLM output without error handling for malformed text.

## Inputs

A JSON generation request record that additionally includes a `constraint` field. The constraint can be one of:
- `json_schema`: a JSON Schema object the output must validate against
- `regex`: a regular expression string the output must match
- `grammar`: a context-free grammar in GBNF or LLGuidance format

## Outputs

An NDJSON stream of constrained tokens and a final completion message, same format as the unconstrained inference caps.

## Notes

- Constraint enforcement happens at the token-sampling level via LLGuidance — the model cannot produce tokens that would violate the constraint.
- Structured output via JSON schema is the most common use case: specify the exact fields and types you need and receive valid JSON every time.
- Performance overhead compared to unconstrained inference is small for simple schemas; complex grammars may add latency.
- Use this cap when building pipelines where a downstream cap expects well-formed JSON and you cannot tolerate parse failures.
- See also: [Make a Decision](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:constrained;in="media:textable";language=en;op=make_decision;out="media:decision;json;record;textable") and [Generate JSON](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:constrained;in="media:textable";language=en;op=generate_json;out="media:json;textable;record") for higher-level constrained tasks.

args.arg_description: The generation request with constraint field (json_schema, regex, or grammar)

output.output_description: NDJSON stream of constrained tokens and completion
